item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
This works for me.

In the phrase "You alright, Goggles?" the "Goggles" could contain a link to On The Seventh Day Of Christmas, a tale that references him.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as described below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Got a chance to speak with Silber about his headcanon regarding this odd little automaton, and got pointed in the direction of the ever loved SCP-1802. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-1502 is a humanoid construct measuring 38cm in height. Its body has been crudely assembled from leather, wire, and bone fragments of unknown sources. The head and upper torso are concealed by a burlap sack fastened with metal wire around the object's waist. Unlike the rest of its body, its arms are mechanical, and have been constructed from surgical steel. The forearms contain an array of tools, including scalpels and a stitching needle, which can be released on folding appendages and locked into place. The left arm also contains a grasping tool which has been hooked to a line of thread running to a spool on its back. The right arm can produce a hypodermic needle which has been connected via plastic tubing to a steel reservoir located underneath the spool of thread.

To this:

SCP-1502 is a humanoid construct measuring 38cm in height. Its body has been crudely assembled from leather, wire, and bone fragments of unknown sources.1 The head and upper torso are concealed by a burlap sack fastened with metal wire around the object's waist. Unlike the rest of its body, its arms are mechanical, and have been constructed from surgical steel. The forearms contain an array of tools, including scalpels and a stitching needle, which can be released on folding appendages and locked into place. The left arm also contains a grasping tool which has been hooked to a line of thread running to a spool on its back. The right arm can produce a hypodermic needle which has been connected via plastic tubing to a steel reservoir located underneath the spool of thread.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Similarity in construction materials between SCP-1502 and SCP-1802 suggests a shared origin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: [DATA EXPUNGED], most likely

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fucking killing me. Very few SCPs have ever actually scared me too badly but for some reason this one really gets to me. The sheer absurdity of the surgery makes this worthy of an upvote, but I must say I really lost interest reading the interview logs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In the last year or so, the Disney channel has aired a sequel series following Corey, Topanga, and Shawn's children. I haven't seen much of it, worrying that it'll turn out to be a hot mess, but I think Feeny shows up from time to time. What would happen if you show the little guy the new show?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait. Wait. Wait wait wait.
What?
I…no. Wait. Really? Really?
But…what? I mean. No. What?

Have a +1. Really.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Exc-fucking-scuse me?
I can't even. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually don't like the core idea. I think it's dumb.

But the interview and everything after that is so good that I'm upvoting. You take a dumb idea and you just keep going and you make it good. Well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My lord, this is amazing. I have never watched Boy Meets World (always turned it off after the intro), and this is still hilarious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*Sigh*

Things like this make me wish I had more time and motivation to actually try and solve the articles which are obvious fun riddles. Great story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First real upvote in about a year. Good job Silber.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, tried to.add on to the SCP to give it more substance and make it more obvious what's going on.

EDIT: I can tell that this is way too opaque, and I can't figure out how to make it less so within the framework of the story, so here's the explanation.

+ show block
– hide block

1502 is a test run by the creator of SCP-1802. It's the consciousnesses of four girls shoved into.a container and given a body and a purpose, then set loose and monitored to see how it works and how the Foundation reacts to it. And that purpose is pretty much a whim of the creator, whatever reasoning he might have had. 1502 is obsessed with it because they were told they were, like 1802 and his inability to "understand much".

In the interview, 1502 seems to be babbling, but it's being as accurate as it can with only sitcom quotes to communicate with, and with its mind so fractured. One of the girls, the Foundation office worker, recognizes Chen, and is freaking out about it. The others don't, but they don't really care. See, just like the subjects will give in to being Feeny (yes, that's almost definitely anomalous, given the "success rate"), half of the girls that are 1502 have accepted being the stitcher, and some haven't ("that wasn't us"). The more accept it, the more stress is put on the remaining girls. The third one gives in during the animal experiment, which is after the interview but before the last update.

The last update is when the girl Chen used to know finally starts to let herself slip. The Foundation doesn't get it, as they wouldn't later with 1802. And she's the odd one out. So the divide finally blurs and fades away, as the secret dies with her. Bits and pieces may surface, but that's all they are. Now it really is just a doll that wants to cut people up.

If you want to know what happened to Chen after this, read On The Seventh Day Of Christmas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't get the last addendum, at all. Is the intended message here that the disappeared women are turning up now as skips, or that Chen is somehow involved in their disappearance, or both, or what?

I find the concept of an involuntary plastic surgery to be interesting, but the rest doesn't do anything for me. Also, the way the surgery is performed shifts focus from "they look like George Feeny now, isn't that horrible?" to "their faces have been horribly mutilated, isn't that horrible?", which is more mundane and less interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
chat reaction 
Close


Dexanote wait
Dexanote wh
Dexanote what the fuck
Dexanote no seriousl
Soulless MinuteBracelet: Now read seasick sharks
Dexanote what in the fuck
MinuteBracelet HE"S PART SHARK
Dexanote the
Dexanote no
Dexanote everyone shut up for a second
Dexanote hold on
Dexanote peers at line
Soulless ?
Dexanote > . The inferred objective of this procedure is to alter the subject's appearance to resemble William Daniels in his role as "Mr. George Feeny" in the television series Boy Meets World2 as closely as possible.
Reject hahaha
Dexanote wh
Dexanote like
Scantron hahahahaha
Dexanote who even thinks of this
SpoonofEvil yeah
SpoonofEvil that is an oddly specific thing to do
Dexanote it's the Seamstress from 9 but
Dexanote what
Silber ahahahahaha
Dexanote wtf
Dexanote no
Soulless lol
MinuteBracelet AND THEY TELL HIM AND HE PUNCHES MYSELF OH MY GOD THIS IS GENIUS
MinuteBracelet SORRY ALL CAPS
Dexanote guys stop laughing
SpoonofEvil I'd hate to be the guy who comes up with that observation
Dexanote i hav
Soulless MinuteBracelet: lol
Scantron the weirdest boner
Jekeled ;c
SpoonofEvil because then I'd get awkward looks all around
Dexanote I haven't been this WTF"d since
SpoonofEvil "how did you know that?"
rumetzen Well time to begin the nightly ritual of watching animated sitcoms on netflix til I pass out at four in the morning
Dexanote when was i this WTF'd
Soulless inb4 Dexanote BSOD
SpoonofEvil "Uhhhh, I'm a really big William Daniels fan?
Dexanote i don'e veen know
Reject that abortion faminepulse wrote, dex
Soulless lol
Dexanote Which abortion
Jekeled ;,
Dexanote and in what sense
Soulless Dexanote: tabula rasa
Soulless !sea tabula
Nala Soulless: Tabula Rasa (rating: +101, 12 revisions) - http://scp-wiki.net/scp-1782
Faminepulse the one in the floorboards
Faminepulse the sink too
Dexanote Oh yeah
Silber The literal abortion
Dexanote yeah
Soulless lol
Dexanote not the really bad
Jekeled ;-;
Dexanote yeah
Dexanote but
Dexanote wh
Dexanote okay fuck it im upvoting then finishing this off

Take my upvote and get out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is absolutely insane and I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added to a few sections in an attempt to bring more context to the situation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the strangeness here, but it doesn't get beyond being "some sort of weird doll", as you put it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh man I both love and hate this. I love it because it's so fantastically weird, and I hate it because it hit so close to an SCP I'm working on in Sandbox (A man who sees every animal as Shia Labeouf. No, seriously.)

I actually skipped to the interview log first, was thoroughly unimpressed, then read from the top down and was thoroughly un-unimpressed.

The only thing I'm not sure about is the entity itself. Some sort of weird doll? As someone else stated before, it's a little bland. It seems like there could be some way to hint at the origins of this thing, or imply some other kind of weird logic or whatever. If there is reasoning for this specific form, it's not coming through, at least to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Less that and more a different mechanism instead of surgical tools.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooh, that's a good idea. It doesn't do it violently. Instead it will attempt to convince you to undergo its procedure. It's (obviously) not very successful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the general idea is pretty good but the whole Feeny thing is pretty ridiculous. I don't know if there's meant to be something more to it, perhaps hidden in the interview, that I missed. No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is it just me or are the containment mesures a bit too vague? We don't know who can or cannot interact with it or do some testing on it - like this, it sounds like anyone can walk in and take that thing out of the containment cell.

That being said… I really like the concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a pretty good concept, but this deserves to be creepier than it is. I would have to argue you could get more coherent quotes for this thing. This article can be shorter.

Also the parallels with other SCPs kind of turn me off from this. I don't know why it should need to be a cosmetic surgeon, or at least a violent one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for just being that weird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I…

I feel like my mind has been violated
This is incredibly fucking unsettling and disturbing like WOW

It's so damn bizarre
How did you even come up with it?? Jesus

It's good, it's really good; or at least I liked it a lot

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I took pains to avoid that show (by which I mean pushing some buttons on a remote).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, is that interview implying that a Foundation doctor made him?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so fucking crazy. Just, so fucking batshit off the walls licking the goddamn windows crazy. this is my newest favorite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh good. This reality was starting to bore me :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A hundred generations from now your words, Lumancer, will bandied about in halls of learning and prayer. A professor, no, a preacher, dressed in the likeness of The Feenie will quote you, and a thousand students gathered around will gnash and hiss at you. How could any doubt, the thrum of their thoughts, a thousand brains thinking the same thing in unison, how could any man doubt what inspired The Prophet Silberescher, the true prophet of the Ultimate Sidereal Universe. It was God, Luma. God touched his brainhole, and the wisdom of the Word came out.

I pray you hide now, Luma, before the revolution starts in earnest, before the decade, no the year, no maybe this hour is out and every government in the world is toppled by a simple people with a simple message of hope and Boy Meets World. I hope for your sake you stay ahead of the crowd. They are wise, no, enlightened people, but they will rend you, cleave you. What Silberescher did today will be a glorious new birth for human civilization, but not one lacking in blood.

In Feeny's Name.

+ show block
– hide block

I just liked this one a lot I guess is my point

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok, Silb? I have to ask- what in HELL inspired this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dunno, this doesn't quite do it for me. The dialog feels like it's saying something, but if it is, I doubt it's something the rest of us have a prayer at figuring out. And without that, it's just kinda generic. This feels almost like something Famine would write, but he wouldn't have it be a pop-culture reference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I absolutely lost it at Mr. Feeny.

And yet I had to give you a very respectful upvote for what I imagine could only be hours poring through Boy Meets World dialogue for that interview. My god.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Begrudging and hateful upvote.

Goddammit, Silber.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The inferred objective of this procedure is to alter the subject's appearance to resemble William Daniels in his role as "Mr. George Feeny" in the television series Boy Meets World as closely as possible.

WHAT.

WHAT?!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
stitching hair from the subject's head over their upper lip

Nope³.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I… what? What? What?

Wait.

…What?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It doesn't talk in Feeny quotes - it talks in quotes from another character on the show. And the surgeries it performs are described as to suggest that they aren't well-executed, e.g. "stitching hair from the subject's head over their upper lip" to create a mustache. It stretches my suspension of disbelief that the Foundation would have seen enough victims to conclusively say "It's trying to make people look like Mr. Feeny."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is written pretty well, but I feel like it doesn't stand on its own unless you are quite familiar with the TV show (which I'm not…)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it simply for the totally weird objective. Like, here's a generic horror construct… which is obsessed with a 90s sitcom. That combination really feels right to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's kinda generic evil doll thing, but with a very specific gimmick which is unnerving enough to raise it above the average Chucky-the-SCP masses.
Also, written well enough, as far as I can tell.

I might upvote… not sure yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It talks in Feeny quotes.

And somebody saw post-op photos of victims and went, "hey you know, they all kinda look like a butchered William Daniels now"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How did the Foundation determine that its primary goal is "to alter the subject's appearance to resemble William Daniels in his role as "Mr. George Feeny" in the television series Boy Meets World2 as closely as possible"? That seems like a pretty significant leap, and isn't explained.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This thing reminds me of 9.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For the interview log, I attribute IMDB; SCP-1502's parts are a collage of lines assembled from th Shawn Hunter quotes page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like this is a rip-off of the plague doctor.
Eh. No vote for now.
